Source_Species.reason: The review is about maize zeins; maize is explicitly stated.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The abstract explicitly gives maize as Zea mays.

Applied_Species.reason: All described experiments and traits are in maize.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: 27-kD γ-zein itself is explicitly discussed as a zein storage protein.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Zeins are explicitly described as storage proteins/prolamins.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Zeins accumulate in protein bodies in maize endosperm; 27-kD γ-zein is part of PB shell/initiation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states 27-kD γ-zein is a storage protein and RNAi indicates it controls PB initiation; high 27-kD γ-zein strongly correlates with QPM vitreousness.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RNAi of 27-kD γ-zein reduced PB number, and QPM sections state higher 27-kD γ-zein correlates with vitreousness; duplication enhances expression in QPM.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The review explicitly cites RNAi suppression, and for QPM cites genetic analysis plus GWAS/linkage/map-based cloning; localization is discussed generally for zein distribution.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is directly summarized in the review.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: γ-zeins are stated to be synthesized before α- and δ-zeins in developing endosperm.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Zein expression is explicitly in developing endosperm.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: γ-zeins are described in the peripheral shell of rough-ER-derived protein bodies.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: 27-kD γ-zein is explicitly a γ-type zein/prolamin.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Kernel development and QPM genetic background are both explicit contexts in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Reduced 27-kD γ-zein decreases PB number, and high 27-kD γ-zein is associated with vitreousness; impairment of vitreousness is directly implied in QPM discussion.

Other_Important_Info.reason: 27-kD γ-zein is a structural zein, not an enzyme or TF; this is consistent with the paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper supports transcription-factor interactions around regulation of the 27-kD γ-zein gene (O2, PBF1, OHPs, ZmbZIP22) and strong zein-zein interactions among γ/β-zeins, but it does not directly state the 27-kD γ-zein protein physically interacts with all listed TFs; protein interaction is clearly supported for 50-, 27-, 16-kD γ-zeins and 15-kD β-zein together, but TFs are regulators of the gene rather than demonstrated protein interactors of the zein protein.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper explicitly states QPM lines can have duplication of the 27-kD γ-zein gene.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: B73 and QPM lines are both explicitly discussed in the review.

Omics_Data.reason: GWAS, linkage mapping, and map-based cloning are explicitly mentioned for QPM/27-kD γ-zein duplication; no accession is given, matching the NA note.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Future perspectives explicitly suggest over-expressing 27-kD γ-zein in opaque high-nutrient mutants to convert opaque endosperm to vitreous.

Source_Species.reason: Maize is explicit throughout the review.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: 22-kD α-zein is explicitly described.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Zeins accumulate in endosperm protein bodies.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper supports a role in PB morphology and as a major storage protein, but specifically says 19-kD α-zeins are mainly responsible for PB filling; assigning PB filling to 22-kD α-zein is not well supported.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RNAi of z1C caused unusual budding PB structures; reduced zein proportion improving lysine/tryptophan is inferable from o2/zein reduction context but not shown specifically for z1C alone.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: RNAi suppression, TEM, and localization-related studies are explicitly discussed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper explicitly states 22-kD α-zeins are encoded by z1C, regulate PB morphology rather than initiation, and RNAi suppression causes abnormal budding PBs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: 22-kD α-zeins are highly expressed in developing endosperm.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: 22-kD α-zeins are described as a discrete ring at the interface between the 19-kD core and γ-zein shell.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: 22-kD α-zein is explicitly an α-zein prolamin.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Kernel development is the context for expression/phenotype.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper supports impaired PB morphology after 22-kD α-zein suppression; opaque texture connection is consistent though indirect.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Fl1 and O10 are explicitly stated to facilitate localization/distribution of 22-kD α-zein.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper discusses the z1C family but does not provide an accession number; 'z1C family' is a family designation, not an accession.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Fl1 and O10 interactions/localization with 22-kD α-zein are explicit; O2, PBF1, and ZmMADS47 are explicit transcriptional regulators of 22-kD α-zein genes.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper states B73 has 16 z1C genes.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome analysis is explicitly described, with no accession provided in the review.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Future perspectives suggest selectively reducing zein-coding genes to improve protein quality; maintaining vitreousness via 27-kD γ-zein is also explicitly discussed.
